/home/samirrav/daos/install/bin/daos_gen_io_conf -a 5 -d 5 testfile -O RP_2G1 -o 10 -s 50

Bootstrapping in parallel sometimes results in more than one rank 0s
Bootstrapping multiple servers in parallel sometimes results in more than one server getting rank 0. This is due to (a) a race between MS creation and Join requests and (b) a missing error check.

Daos rebuild tests failing on VMs, older nodes.
Due to a missed trigger by Jenkins, the DAOS rebuild tests did not actually get added to CI, and this was only noticed today.

On running the job that should have been run, it was discovered that in the first 4 runs, 2 on VMs, and 2 on physical nodes, only one test run passed.

https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/daos-ci_8_node_test-el7/1830/testReport/(root)/ used the new  NVME nodes and it passed.  This was from https://review.hpdd.intel.com/33943

https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/daos-ci_8_vm_test-el7/1834/ failed, triggered from the same review.

Two other test jobs that previously passed were re-run:
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/daos-ci_8_node_test-el7/1829/testReport/  This was using the older physical nodes that appear to be slower than the new NVME nodes.

https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/daos-ci_8_vm_test-el7/1835/ also is using VMs.

The VMs were all updated to day to have 12 GB of ram on them.

cmocka xml output overwritten by avocado cmocka output
For daos_test -p and daos_test -c, the cmocka xml file appears to be overwritten by the upper level avocado test xml

The cmocka output should always take precedence over the avocado output because it can explain why the higher level test failed.

https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/daos-stack/job/daos/job/master/3872/artifact/Functional_Hardware_Medium/daos_test/daos_core_test.py/test-results/03-._daos_test_daos_core_test.py%3ADaosCoreTest.test_daos_pool%3Btransport_config-args-daos_test-scalable_endpoint-stopped_ranks-test_name-transport_config-hosts-pool-0-1-transport_config-timeouts-b748/data/

To compare, for tests that didn't fail, it appears to have the cmocka xml output

https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/daos-stack/job/daos/job/master/3872/artifact/Functional_Hardware_Medium/daos_test/daos_core_test.py/test-results/07-._daos_test_daos_core_test.py%3ADaosCoreTest.test_daos_distributed_tx%3Btransport_config-args-daos_test-scalable_endpoint-stopped_ranks-test_name-transport_config-hosts-pool-0-1-transport_config-timeouts-b748/data/

I suspect maybe it's just using the same name for the file

Types:
Bug
Epic
Story
Sub-Task

src/crt/crt_hg.c:833 crt_hg_reply_send_cb crt_hg_reply_send_cb
We are seeing errors like the below from CaRT on occasion.

02/02-13:04:50.83 lin01 CaRT[7169] CRT  ERR  src/crt/crt_hg.c:833 crt_hg_reply_send_cb crt_hg_reply_send_cb, hg_cbinfo->ret: 4, opc: 0x204.

This happens when we send a direct (non-bulk) rpc where the reply contains a iov which is bigger than the network can support, although the threshold seems to depend on the network MTU so can differ, even for TCP/IP systems.

The above error is reported by the target of the RPC although no errors are detectable by the RPC handler.  On the origin side the completion callback sees cb_info->cci_rc set to --1020 which is -CER_HG, and the iov is NULL, with 0 size.-

We can work around this error, although the current approach is to restrict the amount of data set via iovs.

The ideal fix would be if the network could know the limit in advance, export this information to the application somehow, and return an error from the crt_iov_set() function on the target if the threshold was breached.

I have not tried sending larger iovs with an RPC rather than in the reply.

system reformat fails to unmount rank zero storage
`dmg storage format --reformat` fails after a controlled shutdown since the management service was moved to the control plane. No CI tests were in place for system reformat.

Working hypothesis for the cause of the regression is that now comms with persistent memory is maintained directly from the control-plane based MS even after the IO server processes are terminated during a controlled shutdown, therefore when attempting to reformat storage with daos_server still running, SCM mount for rank 0 (MS) cannot be unmounted, hence storage reformat fails.

    self.uri_file = os.path.join(self.basepath, "install", "tmp", "uri.txt")

This will not work when installed from rpms; should use self.tmp instead

self.uri_file = os.path.join(self.tmp, "uri.txt")

Do rebuild and replica consistency check in logical view perspective
Current rebuild logic will rebuild all physical records from rebuild source to target, if there is VOS aggregation running on the source, the fetch on rebuild will be broken since the prior enumerated records could be removed or coalesced by aggregation.

We can't afford making the rebuild & VOS aggregation mutual exclusion (like abort & prohibit aggregation on rebuild source when start rebuild), because aggregation is for reclaiming space and condensing index, so timely aggregation is crucial for overall system performance (and avoiding out of space error), on the other hand, rebuild is declustered, which means if rebuild and aggregation are exclusive, any single target rebuild will have to prevent many many source targets from being aggregated in time.

To support concurrent rebuild and VOS aggregation, we'd switch rebuild from rebuilding physical records to rebuilding stable logical view which won't be affected by any undergoing aggregation, few stable logical views are:
 # Snapshot view. The snapshot view is stable before it's deleted;
 # Last aggregated epoch view. The last aggregated epoch view is stable before next round of aggregation triggered on higher epoch;

 

The side benefit of logical view rebuild is that it'll transfer less data (only visible records on stable views will be transferred), so rebuild performance will be improved, at the same time, it's not necessary to do aggregation on rebuild target since it'll have aggregated data only.

Replica

Lustre/DAOS Integration efforts for MS164, MS165
MS164 Initial Lustre/DAOS Integration  {color:#FF0000}Due Q3'2019{color}

The Subcontractor shall demonstrate initial integration between Lustre and DAOS. DAOS pools and containers will be represented in the Lustre namespace as regular files with specific extended attributes referencing the relevant DAOS UUID. The DAOS tool(s) will be able to operate on paths to Lustre files and will extract the DAOS UUIDs from the Lustre file or directories.

 

MS165 Advanced Lustre/DAOS Integration and Dataset Mover  {color:#FF0000}Due Q1'2021{color}

The Subcontractor shall demonstrate advanced integration between Lustre and DAOS. DAOS pools and containers will be represented as special files in the Lustre namespace. A FUSE daemon will be implemented to allow read-only access to existing DAOS containers for HSM archiving. New containers can also be created and populated with data from the HSM archive.

 

The Subcontractor shall also demonstrate the dataset mover capability between Lustre and DAOS. The following two options will be supported:
 * A DAOS container can be serialized into a DAOS specific byte stream format that will retain the epoch history, snapshots and DAOS metadata. This DAOS container image can be stored temporarily into a Lustre filesystem and then be deserialized into an existing DAOS pool to be accessed by applications
 * The dataset mover will also support on-the-fly transformation to convert a POSIX, MPI-IO or HDF5 dataset from its DAOS representation

Push event for rebuild and degradation
Server or disk failures occur unexpectedly, so it's better to notify the user by some sort of push event. This isn't possible with CLI without using some script by the user, so I think we should consider adding it for GUI.

For rebuild, we can notify that the rebuild has started, finished, or failed.

For users, we can notify when the container is in degraded mode or data loss occurred. It would also be helpful to notify about server rank shutdown or exclude because user and admin are different group of people.

Note that push events are useful for various important notifications, not just for rebuild and container degradation.

1A Prepare for Soak @ Scale
Existing soak tests modified to scale everything, including storage capacities, duration of individual workloads, number of simultaneous workloads, etc. Test runs for 24 hours.

Tasks identified:
 * Multiple parallel jobs
 * Various middleware io performed in parallel
 * Run with non-replicated, replicated and EC data types
 * Tools used: IOR, mdtest, fio, VPIC IO, LAMMPS
 * Run already available soak tests
 * Modify those tests to adapt to scale everything and run with PBS pro
 * Run for 24hrs

Scope of this ticket is to identify all the soak tasks to be performed for 1A install, identify any extra work required for soak testing during installation and verify the preparation work done is in alignment with overall installation test plan.

Prioritize GC & Aggregation under space pressure
With the improved scheduler in DAOS-4900, aggregation ULTs are now can be prioritized under space pressure, next we need to modify GC to accommodate to the new scheduler:

- Change GC ULT from "per VOS xstream" to "per VOS pool", so that GC ULTs for different pools can be prioritized differently.
- Use new sched API to manage GC ULTs, and put the GC ULT into sleep when there is no pending GC jobs.

Indicate network device name has ambiguity in multiport fabric adapter configuration
The daos_server / dmg network scan returns network device name, provider and NUMA node tuples.  When using fabric adapters, it is possible for there to be more than one network device associated with that fabric adapter.  

I'm exploring ways to definitively describe the network device and provider combinations in this scenario.  An implementation for that, will be another Jira story TBD.  This particular Jira story is an interim step to mark the tuples in some way to show that the network device name is ambiguous.  In this case, it would indicate to the admin that they need to examine their configuration to more carefully understand which network device name correlates to the device they intended to use.

For example, an OPA card identifies as hfi1_0 and has one device associated with hfi1_0 called ib0.  There is no ambiguity that the related device is ib0. 

A Mellanox card may identify as mlx5_0 and have two devices associated with it, (ib0 and enp2s0) for example.  There is ambiguity during the libfabric scan if the mlx5_0 record relates to ib0,or enp2s0. 

Until a comprehensive solution is found to definitively determine the device name, the scan should indicate there is an ambiguity.

daos: preserve cont metadata in daos filesystem copy
Add a --preserve option to the daos filesystem copy. When moving data to POSIX from daos this will serialize all container properties and user attributes to an hdf5, which can be read back in when moving data back to DAOS.

 

Dependencies: This requires hdf5 library access in DAOS as well as serialization/deserialization code to write and read the metadata files. 

Also, DAOS-7843 should really be completed before this PR is merged. 

Need to add serialization of cell size and roots container properties to daos_serialize.c on this PR as well. This code has already been merged into mpifileutils and just needs to be converted over to DAOS serialization library.

MS154 Online Server Addition (SOW)
MS154 Online Server Addition

The Subcontractor shall implement the online server addition feature. The demonstration for this milestone will show a new DAOS server can be added to a pool and that parts of existing datasets will be automatically migrated to the newly added storage. This data rebalancing process will run online while applications are accessing the datasets.

 

*+Deliverables+*

The Subcontractor shall: (1) demonstrate the online server addition feature; (2) deliver an updated version of the DAOS design document if the design has changed; and (3) push the changes to the open source. This milestone will be considered complete when: (1) the Subcontractor delivers updated DAOS design document to Argonne; (2) the Subcontractor presents the design document at the quarterly meeting; and (3) the design document is considered complete to the reasonable satisfaction of Argonne.

Libfabric AV table/FI universe -- HASH support
#1. Creation of the hash structure that is limited in size with ability to evict 'old' entries when hash limit is reached. Hash should support multiple eviction policies, but initially we can start with 'least recently used' (LRU) policy. Performing insertion or lookup operation on a particular entry should increment its LRU count (count here is used broadly, the actual implementation does not need to have a counter). 

Eviction operation should be optional in this capacity limited hash.

Add VMD testing to daos test suite
1. Add VMD devices to current NVMe testing present in daos_nvme_recovery.c:
 * Online faulty reaction
 * Verify device states after NVMe set to faulty
 * Verify blobstore state transition NORMAL->OUT
 * Verify NVMe I/O error and notification
 * Offline faulty reaction
 * Mixed type pool (offline/online) faulty reaction

2. Add mixed devices (NVMe & VMD) to all above test cases

3. Additional VMD specific tests to add:
 * For the verify device states after NVMe set to faulty test case, add test to locate the evicted device (check the LED state is set to fault), and then manually reset the LED to off state
 * Add new test case to locate a VMD device with the dmg storage identify command. Verify the LED state is set to identify for a period of time, and then reset to off. Verify in the mixed devices case that the identify command should fail if the device is not a VMD device.
 * Add new test case to reintegrate an evicted device. This will consist of replacing the device with itself, without actual target reintegration (just essentially brings the device back for use). Needs to be tested for NVMe only, VMD only, and also mixed case. For VMD devices, verify that the LED state goes back to off once the device is replaced. Confirm for all cases that the blobstore state transitions from OUT->NORMAL.

Verify all test cases are being tested in CI!
